articles.title: GSAP v3.13: JavaScript Animation Set Free
articles.description: Last year the popular GSAP (a.k.a. GreenSock) animation library was acquired by Webflow and as of this new version the entire GSAP toolkit is freely available (including formerly paid addons like MorphSVG and SplitText) even for commercial use. If you're unfamiliar with GSAP and want to see some of what it can do, they have a showcase, lots of code demos, and amazing docs.
articles.author: Cassie Evans and Jack Doyle

articles.title: Converting Values to Strings in JavaScript
articles.description: When Dr. Axel says “converting values to strings in JavaScript is more complicated than it might seem”, I’m inclined to believe him. An interesting poke about into something seemingly simple you might not think about very much.
articles.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer

articles.title: 📉 Giving V8 a Heads-Up: Faster Startup with Explicit Compile Hints
articles.description: A look at a V8 optimization that lets you direct V8 to eagerly compile specific files as a way to speed up startup. It ships with Chrome 136 and the performance gains are more compelling than you might expect.
articles.author: Marja Hölttä

articles.title: Export Google Analytics Data to Google Sheets via Apps Script
articles.description: Google Apps Script is a JavaScript-based platform for dynamically automating tasks in all sorts of Google apps. Here’s how to use it to bring Google Analytics data into a Google Sheet.
articles.author: Kayce Basques

articles.title: Building an Offline-Friendly Image Upload System
articles.description: How to leverage PWA tech such as IndexedDB, service workers, and the Background Sync API to help improve the reliability of web apps for users, particularly those with unreliable internet connectivity.
articles.author: Amejimaobari Ollornwi

articles.title: What Does React's 'use client' Do?
articles.description: Dan Abramov takes a practical look at how to think about the use client and use server directives React Server Components introduced and how they let you elegantly structure a client/server app as “a single program spanning two environments” – a concept that Dan thinks could be more widely used, even outside of React.
articles.author: Dan Abramov

articles.title: PDFSlick 3.0: View and Interact with PDF Documents in JS Apps
articles.description: A full-featured PDF viewer for React, Solid, Svelte and vanilla JS apps. Built on top of PDF.js, it offers a wide array of features from simple PDF viewing to working with multiple and large documents with annotations. Demo. v3.0 bumps up to PDF.js v5 with ICC profile support, better JPEG 2000 support, and improved rendering of large pages.
articles.author: Vancho Stojkov

articles.title: Koa 3.0: The Expressive HTTP Middleware Framework
articles.description: Koa first appeared a decade ago as a ‘next-generation’ Web framework that shared some of the lineage of Express.js, but leaning on more modern JS features. While Express has been making a comeback, Koa remains a compelling alternative.
articles.author: Koa contributors

articles.title: Seyfert: A Framework for Building Discord Bots
articles.description: Create bots for the popular chat system starting from bots that respond to simple commands up to creating components and getting user input. Supports Deno, Bun, and Node.
articles.author: socram03

articles.title: Storybook 9 Beta
articles.description: A strong step forward for the UI ‘frontend workshop’ with several experimental features from Storybook 8 becoming stable. v9 has a strong focus on component testing, plus React Native support.
articles.author: Michael Shilman

articles.title: PGlite 0.3: Postgres but in WebAssembly
articles.description: A WebAssembly-based build of the Postgres SQL database meaning you can run it wherever WebAssembly can be run (such as in the browser as in this demo).
articles.author: ElectricSQL

articles.description: Node.js's releases are currently shifting around a little. v18 has just gone 'end of life', v23 has entered 'maintenance' mode and v24 is soon to land as the new 'current' release.
🤠 jQuery was first released in 2006 and to celebrate its 20th anniversary, there's a jQuery Reunion event in Texas on January 16-17, 2026.
The Svelte team has a look at what's new in Svelte lately, and the Astro team has a similar update for Astro too.
You can now install JSR packages with pnpm and Yarn.
Learn about JavaScript's numerous operators with a handy online reference.
Deno 2.3 – The alternative JavaScript runtime improves its single binary compilation feature to support FFI and Node native addons.
Prisma 6.7 – Popular ORM for Node.js and TypeScript.
Bun v1.2.11, Electron 36, pnpm 10.10

articles.title: Easier Layout with margin-trim
articles.description: Jen and the WebKit team have been on something of a roll of late with great blog posts on various modern CSS features, and so it goes with this one on the experimental margin-trim property — a way to tell a container to trim the margins off its children.
articles.author: Jen Simmons

articles.title: Are 'CSS Carousels' Accessible?
articles.description: It’s still early days for the recently introduced CSS Carousels, but that hasn’t stopped Sara from diving in for this excellent look at this experimental feature, sharing an objective look at its current accessibility and general usability.
articles.author: Sara Soueidan

articles.title: The GSAP Web Animation Toolkit is Now Entirely Free
articles.description: Last year the hugely popular GSAP (a.k.a. GreenSock) animation library was acquired by Webflow, and it’s turned out to be great news for us, as from version 3.13 the entire GSAP toolkit is now freely available (including formerly paid addons like MorphSVG and SplitText), even for commercial use. ℹ️ Note: It’s not exactly traditional open source though, using a ‘no charge’ license that prevents you from using it to compete with Webflow directly.
articles.author: Cassie Evans and Jack Doyle

articles.title: Reading Flow Will Ship in Chrome 137
articles.description: Both the reading-flow and reading-order properties are expected to land in the stable version of Chrome later this month — however before then Rachel is seeking folks to try it out and report any bugs found. There's more on this feature over on the Chrome blog.
articles.author: Rachel Andrew

articles.title: WCAG 3.0’s Proposed Scoring Model: A Shift in Accessibility Evaluation
articles.description: There’s a rethink underway in terms of conformance with the web content accessibility guidelines — moving away from a binary success/fail model to one that prioritizes “usability over compliance [..] shifting the focus toward the quality of access rather than the mere presence of features”.
articles.author: Mikhail Prosmitskiy

articles.title: Polyfilling CSS with CSS Parser Extensions
articles.description: Bramus has had an idea “to fix CSS polyfilling once and for all” by allowing developers to extend the browser’s CSS parser with JavaScript.
articles.author: Bramus Van Damme

articles.title: Frontend's Next Evolution: AI-Powered State Management
articles.description: Here’s how artificial intelligence is changing the complexity of state management in modern frontend apps. “Rethinking state management isn’t about discarding what we know. It’s about augmenting it.”
articles.author: Alexander T. Williams

articles.title: Passkeys for Normal People
articles.description: A solid primer on the mechanics of passkeys and the defensive role they play, encouraging us all to adopt this increasingly popular security method.
articles.author: Troy Hunt

articles.title: Masonry in CSS: Should Grid Evolve or Stand Aside for a New Module?
articles.description: It feels like we’ve been talking about this for a while now, and with three proposals in the ring you may have lost sight of where things are — here’s a solid reminder/recap.
articles.author: Gabriel Shoyombo

articles.title: Revisiting Image Maps
articles.description: Although ultimately image maps weren’t the right fit for the task at hand here, this exploration of the feature and the need to find the right tool for the job make for a good read.
articles.author: Andy Clarke

articles.title: Good vs Great Animations
articles.description: A neat collection of practical tips to help you go from good to great animations.
articles.author: Emil Kowalski

articles.title: WebTUI: A Modular CSS Library Bringing the Beauty of Terminal UIs to the Browser
articles.description: You can see it in use on the docs site. Includes ASCII Boxes, a badge, button, checkbox, popover, and other components, along with some plugins.
articles.author: WebTUI

articles.title: Mantine 8.0: The Full Featured React Components Library
articles.description: Mantine is one of the most celebrated React component libraries, and for good reason: it’s full featured, modern, and looks great. v8.0 ups its chart game with a little Recharts magic, adds over twenty new components (including a GitHub-style Heatmap, Tree and SemiCircleProgress), submenus, and much more.
articles.author: Vitaly Rtishchev et al.

articles.title: VERT: A Fast, Ad-Free File Converter for Media & Documents
articles.description: All image formats are converted locally, while videos are sent to a server temporarily. You can also convert documents like XML, MD, HTML, RTF, CSV, JSON and others.
articles.author: vert

articles.title: 9ui: A Curated Library of UI Components Built with Base UI and Tailwind
articles.description: Includes dozens of standard components that are easy to theme and customize, which you can test drive on the theme page.
articles.author: Bora Baloglu

articles.title: Bekuto 3D: Web-Based Tool to Convert SVGs to 3D Models
articles.description: Works well for simple graphics, allowing you to edit the different parts of the file before exporting to a 3D format (STL, GLTTF, or 3MF). GitHub repo. I just tried it with this rocket SVG — works well.
articles.author: Rizumu Ayaka

articles.title: StringTune: A JavaScript Library Designed to Deliver High-Performance, Modular Web Effects
articles.description: This enables parallax scrolling, dynamic cursor interactions, progress tracking, split text animations, and autoplaying videos. You can try out lots of examples on the demo page.
articles.author: fiddle digital
